---
title: "Knowledge Graph ‚Äî Obsidian Plugin Demo"
subtitle: "AI-Powered Study Item Generation & Concept Management"
author: "DSAN 6725 Team"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    slide-number: true
    code-fold: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    highlight-style: github-dark
    transition: slide
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    footer: "Knowledge Graph Plugin v0.1.0"
---

## What We Built {.center}

An **Obsidian plugin** backed by a **FastAPI server** that turns your
personal notes into an AI-driven study system.

- Write concepts as markdown notes in your vault
- The plugin reads your knowledge graph (concepts + edges)
- An LLM pipeline generates study items tailored to **your** knowledge
- You answer, get graded, and progress through difficulty tiers

---

## Architecture Overview {.smaller}

Two feedback cycles drive every round. **Solid** = data flow, **dashed** = feedback edge.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 12
%%| fig-height: 4.5
flowchart LR
    %% --- Sample item evaluation (pre-round) ---
    Sample["Sample item<br/>(md / image)"] ==> SE["<b>Sample Evaluator</b><br/>multimodal pre-grading"]
    SE -. "analysis_notes" .-> Gen

    %% --- Core hardening loop (during round) ---
    Gen["<b>Generator</b>"] ==> Act["<b>Actor √ó3</b><br/>simulated solutions"]
    Act ==> Ref["<b>Reflector</b><br/>quality + hardening"]
    Ref -. "hardening directives<br/>(if difficulty ‚â• hard)" .-> Gen
    Ref ==> Items["Items in vault"]

    classDef phase fill:#1e293b,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#e2e8f0,stroke-width:2px;
    classDef data  fill:#0f172a,stroke:#475569,color:#cbd5e1,stroke-width:1px;
    class SE,Gen,Act,Ref phase
    class Sample,Items data
```

*Per-concept Answer Evaluator (post-reply ‚Üí Elo mastery + `focus_depth`) is detailed two slides on.*

---

## The Generation Pipeline {.smaller}

A round chains a **pre-grading pass + 5-stage loop + per-concept answer grading**, all via LLM tool-calling:

| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| **Sample Evaluator** *(pre-round, multimodal)* | Pre-grades sample reference (md / image) ‚Üí `analysis_notes` for Generator |
| **Feasibility** | Gates whether requested concepts √ó difficulty is generatable |
| **Generator** | Produces N items from concepts, edges, examples, images, `analysis_notes` |
| **Actor** √ó3 | Simulated students solve each item in parallel |
| **Reflector** | Scores quality, drives refinement (+hardening for hard+) |
| **Round Evaluator** | Summarizes round quality, supports difficulty progression |
| **Answer Evaluator** *(per-concept)* | Verdicts `correct / alt_path / misunderstood / untested` ‚Üí Elo mastery |

Structured tool-calling (Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5) ‚Äî no free-text concatenation.

---

## Iterative Hardening (hard / very_hard / expert) {.smaller}

For difficulty ‚â• **hard**, items go through up to 3 hardening iterations:

1. Generate items at **medium** difficulty first
2. **Actor** solves them ‚Äî produces reasoning trajectories
3. **Hardening Reflector** analyzes trajectories:
   - "Actor solved trivially ‚Üí add multi-step reasoning"
   - "High confidence ‚Üí inject edge cases"
   - "Few concepts used ‚Üí require cross-concept synthesis"
4. Reflector also pulls **random extra concepts** from your graph
5. **Generator** re-creates items with hardening directives
6. Repeat ‚Äî `item(i+1) = f(item(i))`, single-slot context (no accumulation)

---

## Five Difficulty Tiers {.smaller}

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 8
flowchart LR
    E["Easy"] --> M["Medium"] --> H["Hard\n(+hardening)"]
    H --> VH["Very Hard\n(+hardening)"]
    VH --> EX["Expert\n(+hardening)"]

    style E fill:#4CAF50,color:#fff
    style M fill:#FFC107,color:#000
    style H fill:#FF9800,color:#fff
    style VH fill:#F44336,color:#fff
    style EX fill:#9C27B0,color:#fff
```

- User **manually selects** difficulty ‚Äî no forced auto-escalation
- Hard+ triggers the iterative hardening loop above
- Extra concepts from your knowledge graph make items harder but solvable

---

## Plugin UI ‚Äî Context Images & PDF Upload {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Inline Context Images

Attach exam paper images as visual context:

- **Attach Image** ‚Äî file picker (png, jpg, webp)
- **From Vault** ‚Äî pick images in your vault

Images are base64-encoded and sent **inline** with
the tool-calling JSON ‚Äî Claude sees the image alongside
concept context when generating items.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### PDF ‚Üí Concept Graph

Upload a PDF paper:

1. **MinerU** extracts text to markdown
2. **LLM** segments into concept nodes + directed edges
3. Notes auto-created in vault with YAML frontmatter
4. Concepts + edges auto-populated in the generator form

Instantly turns any paper into a study-ready knowledge graph.
:::

::::

---

## Section Graph Builder (Link-Architect) {.smaller}

Yubo's pipeline turns a MinerU-extracted paper into a typed directed graph that the depth-aware scheduler reads from. Adapted from his `karpathy_agents/` (`origin/yubo`) by keeping **only** the link-architect step.

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Pipeline

1. `MinerU` ‚Üí markdown + assets
2. `section_splitter` ‚Üí `Section` list (one per `##`)
3. `graph_builder.build_section_graph(sections)` ‚Äî **one LLM call per paper** (`chat_json_completion`, JSON-mode)
4. Tarjan SCC condenses cycles ‚Üí topological layering
5. `(edges, depths)` written to `paper_index_service`
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Outputs (`GraphResult`)

```python
edges: list[GraphEdge]         # source, target, label, anchor
depths: dict[section_id, int]  # 0 = root layer
```

Edge labels: `depends_on`, `contrasts`, `generalizes`, `instantiates` (directionality: *source elaborates / depends on target*).

Gracefully degrades ‚Äî no LLM key returns `(edges=[], depths={s: 0})` so PDF ingest never breaks.
:::

::::

`depths` feeds the scheduler's `focus_depth` cursor; `edges` populate the generator's foundation context.

---

## Session Resume & Sample Items {.smaller}

### Resume
- Session state lives in the **markdown note** (YAML frontmatter)
- Pick any `Items/*.md` from the dropdown ‚Üí resume generating rounds
- Survives Obsidian restarts, backend restarts ‚Äî fully stateless

### Sample Items
- `SampleItems/` folder in your vault
- Paste exam problems as reference items
- Plugin reads them and feeds to generator as `example_items`
- Generator mimics the style/format of your samples

---

## Answer Grading {.smaller}

The plugin reads your answers from markdown callouts and grades them:

````markdown
#### Your Answer

> [!quote]- Write your answer here
> P(A|B) = P(B|A) * P(A) / P(B)
> Using the law of total probability for the denominator...

#### Evaluator Feedback  ‚Üê injected by plugin
> [!success] Score: 8.5/10
> Strong application of Bayes' theorem.
> Consider explicitly stating the partition used in the denominator.
````

- LLM compares your answer against the reference solution
- Feedback injected directly into the note
- Scores visible before choosing next round difficulty

---

## Offline Evaluation Harness (Braintrust) {.smaller}

Yubo's `evals/item_generation/eval.py` ships alongside the code so any reviewer can re-run the pilot. **Generation runs outside the Braintrust event loop** to avoid nested `asyncio.run()` conflicts; `Eval()` only scores recorded outputs.

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Scorer families

- **Gates** ‚Äî `NoRunError`, `FeasibilityAlign`, `ItemStructural`, `OutputNonEmpty`
- **Heuristics** *(`extra_metrics.py`)* ‚Äî `FoundationCoverage`, `ProblemPromptShape`, `BodyLengthBand`, `AnswerLength`, `MarkdownStructure`
- **Cost / latency** ‚Äî `ProxyTokenEfficiency` (tiktoken-based), wall-clock band score
- **LLM judges** *(`llm_judge_scorers.py`, `autoevals.LLMClassifier`)* ‚Äî `RubricSatisfaction`, `ItemClarity` with packed rubric blocks
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Pilot ($N=2$) ‚Äî descriptive only

| Scorer | Mean |
|---|---|
| `NoRunError` / `FeasibilityAlign` / `OutputNonEmpty` / `FoundationCoverage` / `ProblemPromptShape` / `AnswerLength` / `BodyLengthBand` / `ProxyTokenEfficiency` | **100%** |
| `RubricSatisfaction` (judge) | **91%** |
| `Latency` (band) | **87.5%** |
| `ItemStructural` | **85%** |
| `ItemClarity` (judge) | 69% |
| `MarkdownStructure` | 46% |

`--skip-llm-judges` and `--no-send-logs` flags for offline / sandboxed runs.
:::

::::

---

## Tech Stack {.smaller}

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| **Frontend** | Obsidian plugin (TypeScript, esbuild) |
| **Backend** | FastAPI (Python 3.11+, async) |
| **LLM** | Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 (tool-calling) |
| **LLM Fallback** | Groq (OpenAI-compatible, free tier) |
| **PDF Extraction** | MinerU (`mineru[pipeline]`) |
| **Data Format** | Markdown + YAML frontmatter |
| **Session State** | Stateless ‚Äî note IS the session |

---

## What's Next {.smaller}

- **Concept CRUD** ‚Äî full backend persistence (MongoDB)
- **Embedding pipeline** ‚Äî semantic search via Pinecone
- **Graph visualization** ‚Äî Cytoscape.js panel in Obsidian
- **Traversal engine** ‚Äî KST outer-fringe personalized paths
- **Group landscapes** ‚Äî merged canonical views across users
- **Standalone web UI** ‚Äî move beyond Obsidian for broader access

---

## Demo Time {.center}

Let's generate some study items live.

:::{.callout-tip}
### Try it yourself
1. Open Obsidian with the plugin installed
2. Click the brain icon in the left ribbon
3. Import your concept notes
4. Hit **Generate Items**
:::
